page: Amazon Web Services c6a.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with an x86_64 architecture, offering 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with a base clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The memory configuration consists of 256 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU tests and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with small-block cached read operations placing in the top 10% and large-block uncached read/write operations in the top 25%. Software compilation via Clang and ray tracing workloads also achieve top-tier performance. However, single-core performance and text processing benchmarks fall into the average tier, indicating that the instance's primary strength lies in parallelized multi-threaded execution.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c6a.32xlarge offers high compute density for parallel processing without the added cost of bundled local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators. This makes it a highly specialized option for workloads that do not require local disk persistence or hardware acceleration. The instance is suited for compute-heavy workloads such as high-performance computing, batch processing, software compilation, distributed analytics, and multi-threaded web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c6a.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro System, featuring an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 128 vCPUs, 64 physical cores, and a 3.6 GHz clock speed. It is equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and features a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, prioritizing raw compute density. Benchmark results place its multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU score, and software compilation capabilities in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth for cached reads is also top-tier, while single-core performance remains in the average range. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for parallelized, compute-intensive tasks that rely on network-attached storage, making it suitable for software compilation, batch processing, distributed analytics, and high-performance computing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, 256 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for highly parallelized workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth for parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-density, multi-core compute instance designed for parallel processing, software compilation, and network-heavy distributed workloads without local storage overhead.
bullet_points: Powered by a 128-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz
Equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory at a 2.0 GB per core ratio
Delivers 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, software compilation, and memory bandwidth benchmarks
Optimized for parallel batch processing, distributed analytics, and HPC workloads
